comment.body:
simply bcs sellers want to sell high, buyers want to buy low.
bulls = buy low sell high later
bears = sell high, buyback low

same thing, two sides

user.name: Alma
user.bio: Privat investor & speculator | Mathematics | Self-made quant | Market researcher | Options and Futures trader

comment.body:
when market goes up, it is bcs sellers can haggle out even higher prices to sell at bcs buyers estimate that their trade will be profitable (meaning: market will go up more).
and when profit taking occurs, bcs spot reaches a level where buyers are not interested too much temporarly (the majority of them), they start to close their trades, get stopped out, and spot goes down.

now, sellers look for the buyers, they don't want to go too much lower, but they need to meet, you know? logical.
so they test what are the prices that the buyers accepts
comment.raw_body:
